title: 10 best herbal creams for fluid retention

remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Canadian thistle creams help reduce fluid retention by increasing urine production, allowing excess water and electrolytes to be eliminated from the body.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Canadian thistle creams have anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with fluid retention, making it easier for the body to manage excess fluids.

Sodium-reducing properties: Canadian thistle creams contain compounds that can help regulate sodium levels in the body, which can contribute to fluid retention. By reducing sodium absorption, these creams help reduce fluid buildup in the tissues and promote healthy blood pressure.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cirsium arvense, also known as Canada thistle, creams helps with fluid retention because of its diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to enhance urine production and reduce fluid buildup in the body. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help to alleviate swelling and discomfort associated with fluid retention. By promoting the elimination of excess fluids and reducing inflammation, Cirsium arvense creams provide a natural and effective solution for managing fluid retention and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have been found to have diuretic properties, which help in increasing urine production and reducing fluid retention in the body.

Triterpenoids: Certain triterpenoids present in Cirsium arvense, such as ursolic acid, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects and may help in reducing water retention by improving kidney function.
Saponins: Saponins in Cirsium arvense may act as a natural diuretic, helping to increase urine output and reduce fluid buildup in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their diuretic properties, which help to reduce fluid retention by increasing urine production.
Flowers: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate fluid retention.
Leaves: They are used due to their diuretic and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce fluid retention and alleviate pain associated with it.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of cirsium arvense flowers in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm oven at 150°f for 2 hours to preserve their potency.
Combine 2 tablespoons of dried flowers with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes and then strain it through a cheesecloth to separate the infused oil.
Whip 1/4 cup of beeswax with 1 tablespoon of the infused oil to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow creams contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and lubricates the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by excess fluids in the body.
Saponins: Herbal marshmallow creams are rich in saponins, a type of triterpene that helps to reduce water retention by increasing urine production and improving kidney function.
Astringent properties: Marshmallow creams have astringent properties, which help to tighten and shrink swollen tissues, reducing the appearance of puffiness and fluid buildup in the body.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, creams helps with fluid retention because of its unique properties. The root of the plant contains mucilages, which are complex carbohydrates that have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects. When applied topically, the mucilages help to reduce swelling and ease inflammation in the skin, allowing it to function normally and reduce fluid buildup. This natural remedy can also help to improve skin elasticity and reduce water retention, promoting a more even and healthy complexion.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages in Althaea officinalis creams help with fluid retention by forming a protective barrier on the skin, reducing inflammation, and promoting the elimination of excess fluids.

Gallic acid (a phenolic acid): Gallic acid in Althaea officinalis creams helps with fluid retention by reducing inflammation, improving blood circulation, and preventing the reabsorption of excess fluids in the body.

Triterpenes (specifically ursolic acid): Triterpenes in Althaea officinalis creams help with fluid retention by reducing inflammation, promoting diuresis (the production of urine), and improving the body's ability to eliminate excess fluids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part of Althaea officinalis for creams due to their high mucilage content, which helps to reduce fluid retention and soothe skin irritations.

Leaves: The leaves are used to make creams for fluid retention because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, helping to reduce swelling and excess fluid.

Barks: The barks are occasionally used to make creams for fluid retention, as they contain tannins and other compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and promote the elimination of excess fluid.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried althaea officinalis root from a trusted supplier or cultivate it in a controlled environment.
Combine the dried root in a double boiler with 100ml of distilled water and gently heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container to remove any residue.
Mix 50 grams of cetyl alcohol with 10 grams of stearic acid in a separate container to create a wax blend.
Blend the wax blend into the cooled althaea officinalis infusion and whip until a smooth cream forms.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which help reduce swelling and inflammation caused by fluid retention.
Antioxidants: Nettle's antioxidants, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, neutralize free radicals that can contribute to fluid buildup in the body and improve overall circulation.
Diuretic properties: Stinging nettle has been traditionally used as a natural diuretic, helping to increase urine production and promote the elimination of excess fluids from the body.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, creams helps with fluid retention because of its diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, work to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, helping the body to remove excess fluids and toxins. By reducing swelling and improving lymphatic drainage, Urtica dioica creams promote the natural elimination of fluids, providing relief from water retention, swelling, and discomfort associated with edema and other fluid-related conditions.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids (specifically, stachydrine and trigonelline): These alkaloids help reduce fluid retention by increasing urine production and improving kidney function, thereby facilitating the removal of excess fluids from the body.
Saponins: Saponins in Urtica dioica creams help alleviate fluid retention by reducing inflammation and improving lymphatic drainage, which aids in the removal of excess fluids from the body.

Phenolic acids (specifically, caffeic acid and ferulic acid): These phenolic acids exhibit diuretic properties, helping to reduce fluid retention by increasing urine production and improving the body's ability to remove excess fluids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh urtica dioica leaves in early morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure minimal moisture content.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer on paper towels or screens at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Grind the dried urtica dioica leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill for 30 seconds.
Mix 10 grams of the powder with 50 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler at low heat.
Stir the mixture constantly for 10 minutes until it reaches a smooth consistency then pour into a tin container and let cool.

remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Horsetail contains flavonoids, which increase urine production and help remove excess water from the body, reducing fluid retention.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants in horsetail cream reduce inflammation and swelling, alleviating the discomfort associated with fluid retention and other related conditions like edema.
Sodium and potassium balancing properties: Horsetail's mineral content helps regulate sodium and potassium levels in the body, which can help balance water retention and reduce swelling.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense, also known as field horsetail, creams helps with fluid retention because of its diuretic properties and ability to improve circulation. By increasing urine production and reducing water retention, Equisetum arvense creams can provide relief from conditions such as edema, bloating, and swelling. Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Equisetum arvense, such as taxifolin and isorhapontigenin, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that contribute to fluid retention.

Saponins: Saponins in Equisetum arvense may help with fluid retention by increasing urine production and reducing the amount of sodium reabsorbed by the kidneys, leading to a decrease in fluid retention.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Equisetum arvense are commonly used to make creams due to their diuretic properties, which help reduce fluid retention in the body.
Stems: The stems of Equisetum arvense are often used to create creams because they contain compounds that aid in reducing swelling and inflammation associated with fluid retention.
Leaves: The leaves of Equisetum arvense are utilized in creams to address fluid retention due to their ability to stimulate urination and promote the removal of excess fluids from the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of fresh equisetum arvense roots and leaves from a clean environment.
Steep 5 grams of the harvested plant material in 100ml of boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a bowl to separate the plant material.
Combine the strained liquid with 10 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously over low heat until the beeswax and coconut oil are fully incorporated and the mixture has cooled to a solid consistency.

remedy_properties: Diuretic Properties: Dandelion root and leaves contain compounds that stimulate urine production, helping to reduce water retention and alleviate swelling. This is due to the presence of taraxasterol, a triterpene saponin that increases urine flow.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: The flavonoids and terpenes present in dandelion extracts have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce edema (swelling) caused by fluid retention. This is because these compounds inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory molecules and enzymes.

Choleretic Properties: Dandelion root has been shown to increase bile production and flow, which can help reduce water retention in the body. This is achieved through the stimulation of the gallbladder to release more bile into the small intestine, which aids digestion and absorbs excess fluids.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with fluid retention because of its diuretic properties. The roots and leaves of the plant contain compounds that stimulate blood vessels and promote the removal of excess fluids from the body. This natural diuretic action can help reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms associated with fluid retention, such as bloating and discomfort. By promoting the elimination of excess fluids, dandelion creams can provide relief from fluid retention and support overall well-being. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Taraxacum officinale help reduce fluid retention by improving the body's ability to excrete excess water and electrolytes.
Coumarins: Coumarins in Taraxacum officinale may help reduce fluid retention by inhibiting the reabsorption of sodium and water in the kidneys, thereby promoting diuresis.
Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol in Taraxacum officinale may help reduce fluid retention by modulating the renin-angiotensin system, which is involved in regulating blood pressure and fluid balance.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their diuretic properties, which help increase urine production and reduce fluid retention.
Roots: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, which help alleviate swelling and excess fluid in the body.
Flowers: They are used due to their diuretic properties and as a natural alternative to pharmaceutical diuretics, helping to reduce fluid retention and alleviate swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh dandelion flowers at peak bloom for optimal potency and store them in an airtight container.
Combine 1 cup of dried dandelion flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Blend the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds: Reduce swelling and edema caused by fluid retention, making it easier for the body to process excess fluids.
Anthraquinones: Help to improve digestion and reduce water retention by stimulating bowel movements and preventing constipation.
Vasodilatory effects of aloe gel: Dilate blood vessels, reducing pressure on the kidneys and allowing them to excrete excess fluid more efficiently.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with fluid retention because of its natural diuretic properties and ability to improve lymphatic circulation. The gel-like substance within aloe vera contains compounds such as aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to reduce inflammation and promote the removal of excess fluids from the body. Regular application of aloe vera creams can also help to soften and break down connective tissue, allowing for better fluid drainage and reduction in water retention. This results in a more even and healthy skin appearance.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds, particularly aloe-emodin and aloin, have been shown to increase urine production and reduce water retention by stimulating the kidneys to remove excess fluids from the body.

Flavonoids (particularly Aloe-emodin): Aloe-emodin has been found to have diuretic properties, which help to increase urine production and reduce fluid retention by acting on the kidneys and blood vessels to promote the removal of excess fluids.

Terpenoids (particularly alpha-humulene and beta-sitosterol): These compounds may help reduce fluid retention by improving blood circulation, reducing inflammation, and promoting lymphatic drainage, which in turn helps to remove excess fluids from the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in compounds like aloe-emodin, which helps reduce fluid retention by promoting the removal of excess water from the body.
Gel from Leaves: Aloe vera gel, extracted from the inner part of the leaves, is known for its anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, which help alleviate fluid retention symptoms.
Aloin from Leaves: A natural anthraquinone found in aloe vera leaves, aloin helps to stimulate bowel movements, promoting the elimination of excess fluids and reducing fluid retention.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of aloe vera gel from mature leaves and store it in the refrigerator for later use.
Combine 100g of aloe vera gel with 20g of shea butter and 20g of coconut oil in a mixing bowl.
Melt the shea butter and coconut oil in a double boiler for 10 minutes or until fully liquefied.
Blend the mixture until smooth using a hand mixer or whisk for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and let it cool and set for 2 hours before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Purple coneflower cream reduces inflammation, which helps alleviate symptoms of fluid retention by decreasing swelling and discomfort.
Diuretic properties: The herbal extract in purple coneflower cream stimulates the kidneys to increase urine production, helping to remove excess fluids and reduce water retention.

remedy_parts: Roots: Echinacea purpurea roots are commonly used due to their high concentration of inulin, which helps to reduce fluid retention.

Flowers: Echinacea purpurea flowers are used for their antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate fluid retention symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh flowers of echinacea purpurea, ideally in the morning when the dew is still present.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steep 10 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers in 100 milliliters of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to create a liquid extract.
Mix 5 milliliters of the echinacea extract with 10 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 10-15 minutes at 160-180 degrees fahrenheit to create a smooth and creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Allantoin: Helps to reduce inflammation and promote collagen synthesis, which can aid in reducing water retention by improving lymphatic circulation and reducing swelling.
Mucilage: Forms a protective barrier on the skin's surface, trapping excess moisture and preventing it from being reabsorbed into the body, thereby reducing fluid retention.
Tannins: Have a diuretic effect, which can help to increase urine production and eliminate excess fluids in the body, thereby reducing water retention.

Iridoid glycosides: Contribute to reducing fluid retention by increasing urine production and improving kidney function, which helps to eliminate excess water from the body.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help reduce swelling and edema associated with fluid retention.

remedy_recipe: Infuse 4 ounces of dried symphytum officinale in 16 ounces of oil at 100°f for 2 to 3 weeks.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal extract.
Mix 2 ounces of the herbal extract with 8 ounces of beeswax and 8 ounces of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 180°f for 30 to 40 minutes to melt the wax.
Remove the mixture from heat and allow it to cool and thicken before transferring to a container.

Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce fluid retention by improving the body's ability to absorb and remove excess fluids, as well as by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy blood vessel function.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers on a sunny day when they are in full bloom for 2 hours.
Dry the flowers in a warm oven at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 1 hour or until crispy.
Infuse 1 cup of dried flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids for 30 minutes.
Mix 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter for 10 minutes.

Crush 10 grams of dried plantago major leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.

Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and let it cool before transferring it to a container for storage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried ammi majus flowers at 30 grams and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground ammi majus powder with coconut oil at 100 milliliters and beeswax at 20 grams in a double boiler on low heat for 20 minutes.
Stir the mixture constantly until the beeswax melts and the temperature reaches 60 degrees celsius over the course of 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to room temperature while stirring occasionally over a period of 30 minutes.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized jars at 250 milliliters capacity and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with fluid retention because of its natural ability to stimulate digestion and relieve constipation. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried foeniculum vulgare leaves in late summer when essential oil content is highest.
Chop the harvested foeniculum vulgare leaves into small pieces and dry them using a food dehydrator for 2 hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves with 40 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks at room temperature, shaking daily to facilitate herbal extraction into the oil.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard solids before transferring the infused oil to airtight containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with fluid retention because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has potent anti-inflammatory properties. Flavonoids: Licorice cream contains flavonoids, a class of plant compounds that have natural diuretic effects, increasing urine production and reducing excess fluid buildup in the body.

Amino acids: The amino acids present in licorice root, such as glycine and glutamic acid, help regulate sodium levels in the body, which can contribute to fluid retention by reducing excessive water absorption.

remedy_recipe: Weigh 100g of glycerin and mix it with 50g of distilled water in a heat-resistant container.
Combine 20g of dried licorice root powder with 10g of vitamin e oil in a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Add the mixture from step 2 to the glycerin and water mixture, heating it gently until dissolved.
Stir in 30g of beeswax and 10g of sweet almond oil, continuing to heat until melted and well combined.
Allow the mixture to cool to room temperature before whipping with a hand mixer for 5 minutes.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Hydrangea extracts have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling and edema associated with fluid retention.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality hydrangea arborescens leaves and flowers, ensuring freshness for optimal potency and efficacy within hours of harvest.
Combine 200g dried hydrangea arborescens herbs with 1000ml distilled water in a large glass container, allowing mixture to steep for 4 hours under shade.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, discarding solids and retaining approximately 700ml of infused extract.
Mix the extracted liquid with 300g beeswax, 100g coconut oil, and 20g shea butter in a heat-proof bowl, gently warming the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes until fully incorporated.
Pour the warm mixture into clean glass jars or tins, allowing it to cool and thicken at room temperature within 30-45 minutes before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Juniper creams helps with fluid retention because of their natural diuretic properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh juniperus communis leaves and stems and dry them in a well-ventilated area for 3 days.
Combine 20 grams of dried juniperus communis with 100ml of olive oil in a heatproof glass bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to infuse the oil with the herbal properties.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and setting aside for later use.
Mix the strained oil with 100ml of coconut cream and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler until the mixture thickens to a smooth consistency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marrubium vulgare leaves and dry them in a warm and well-ventilated area for 7 days.
Combine 20 grams of dried marrubium vulgare with 100 grams of petroleum jelly and 50 grams of coconut oil.
Mix the ingredients in a heat-resistant bowl using a double boiler method over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids after 15 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh phellodendron amurense leaves measuring 2 tablespoons of dried or 4 tablespoons of fresh leaves.
Combine the harvested leaves with 1 cup of boiling water for 10-15 minutes allowing for infusion and extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a bowl collecting the liquid extract and discarding solids.
Mix the extracted liquid with 8 ounces of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of beeswax melting them together until smooth and emulsified.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars filling to the brim allowing it to cool set and thicken at room temperature.

Combine 20 grams of dried rosa rugosa fruit with 200 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a heat-proof glass bowl.
Steam the mixture over boiling water for precisely 30 minutes to facilitate effective extraction.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean, sterilized glass container and discard solids after cooling to room temperature.
Whip 100 grams of cold-pressed coconut cream with an electric mixer until smooth and creamy, then gently fold in the rosa rugosa oil preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with fluid retention because of its unique ability to stimulate liver function and promote the body's natural detoxification processes. Flavonoids: Flavonoids in milk thistle cream have vasodilatory properties, which can help dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing pressure on the body's tissues and allowing excess fluids to be eliminated more effectively.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried silybum marianum leaves and flowers in early morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using shade drying or air circulation method within 24 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix the powdered silybum marianum with emulsifying wax and carrier oils in a 1:3 ratio for optimal blending.
Heat the mixture at low temperature of 60°c to 70°c for 30 minutes to facilitate infusion and homogenization.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for fluid retention are topical preparations containing plant extracts that help alleviate fluid accumulation in the body, reducing swelling and discomfort. The best herbal creams for fluid retention include Urtica dioica, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Silybum marianum, Centella asiatica, Astragalus membranaceus, Taraxacum officinale, Ruscus aculeatus, Verbascum thapsus, Aloe barbadensis, and Plantago lanceolata. If left untreated, fluid retention can cause fatigue, bloating, and skin issues, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to fluid retention that are treated with herbal creams are cellulite, edema, and varicose veins. Herbal creams containing ingredients like horse chestnut, butcher's broom, and witch hazel help to reduce swelling, improve circulation, and strengthen vein walls. Other herbs such as ginkgo biloba and gotu kola may also be included to enhance their effectiveness in alleviating these conditions. These natural remedies provide an alternative or complement to traditional treatments for these common issues.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with fluid retention is a blend of Dandelion root cream and Witch Hazel cream. Dandelion root cream helps to reduce water retention by promoting the removal of excess fluids from the body. Witch Hazel cream, on the other hand, reduces inflammation and improves circulation, helping to prevent fluid buildup. When used together, these creams can effectively alleviate fluid retention, reduce swelling, and provide relief from discomfort and pain associated with excess fluids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica creams helps with fluid retention because they are rich in diuretic compounds that help to eliminate excess fluids from the body. The cream's active ingredients, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to improve lymphatic drainage and reduce inflammation in the body. This helps to remove toxins and excess fluids that can contribute to swelling and fluid retention. As a result, the cream can help to reduce swelling and promote a more even body tone.
remedy_constituents: Sterols: Help with fluid retention by reducing inflammation and improving the permeability of blood vessels, thus preventing fluid buildup in tissues.
Triterpenes: Aid in fluid retention by acting as natural diuretics, increasing urine production and reducing the amount of fluid retained in the body.
Flavonoids: Contribute to fluid retention management by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling and improve lymphatic drainage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of diuretic compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce fluid retention.
Roots: The roots are used for their anti-inflammatory properties and diuretic effects, which can help alleviate swelling and promote fluid drainage.
Stems: The stems of Urtica dioica contain diuretic compounds like iridoid glycosides, which contribute to their effectiveness in reducing fluid retention.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems from a clean environment on a dry morning.
Clean and dry the harvested urtica dioica thoroughly using a fine mesh strainer and paper towels within 30 minutes.
Combine 1 cup of dried urtica dioica with 2 cups of carrier oil such as olive oil in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the liquid to a clean glass container within 15 minutes.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax with 1 tablespoon of cocoa butter and 2 tablespoons of carrier oil from step 3 then heat and whip until smooth within 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with fluid retention because they contain licorice extract, which has natural diuretic properties. The active compound, glycyrrhizin, is known to help reduce inflammation and relax the body's blood vessels. This relaxation allows for the more efficient removal of excess fluids from the body, reducing swelling and bloating. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been shown to stimulate the kidneys to produce more urine, further aiding in the elimination of excess fluids and promoting a more balanced body composition.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds contribute to the reduction of fluid retention by inhibiting the activity of the enzyme 11β-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, which is involved in the conversion of cortisol to its active form, thus reducing water retention.

Glycyrrhizin: This compound helps with fluid retention by reducing the activity of aldosterone, a hormone that regulates electrolyte balance and water retention in the body, thereby minimizing water accumulation in tissues.

Flavonoids: These compounds exhibit diuretic properties and help in reducing fluid retention by promoting the elimination of excess water from the body through increased urine production and improved kidney function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are commonly used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce water retention and alleviate swelling.
Leaves: Leaves are also used for their antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate fluid retention symptoms.
Seeds: Seeds contain compounds that aid in reducing fluid retention, including flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to improve circulation and reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier and soak them in 500ml of hot water.
Steep the soaked roots for 30 minutes then strain the liquid to obtain a strong root extract.
Combine 200g of a base cream with 50g of the extracted root liquid and 10g of a preservative in a mixing bowl.
Whisk the mixture for 5 minutes until it reaches a smooth and consistent texture using a hand mixer.
Pour the final mixture into a clean container and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams helps with fluid retention because they contain flavonoids and silymarin, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds work together to improve the body's ability to eliminate excess fluids and electrolytes, reducing swelling and discomfort. The silymarin in Silybum marianum creams also helps to strengthen the tissues and improve circulation, further aiding in the reduction of fluid retention. This makes Silybum marianum creams a popular natural remedy for alleviating symptoms of fluid retention.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: Silymarin helps with fluid retention by reducing inflammation and promoting the removal of excess fluids from the body through its diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Lignans: Lignans help with fluid retention by inhibiting the reabsorption of sodium and water in the kidneys, thereby increasing urine production and reducing fluid accumulation in the body.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids help with fluid retention by improving blood circulation and reducing the permeability of blood vessels, which helps to reduce fluid buildup in the body and alleviate edema.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their diuretic properties, which help reduce fluid retention.
Leaves: They are used due to their ability to stimulate diuresis and reduce inflammation, contributing to the alleviation of fluid retention.

remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum seeds from mature plants in the summer season when they are fully ripe.
Dry the seeds in a warm place for 1 week or by using a food dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius.
Grind the dried seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the powder with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 10 minutes or until it reaches a temperature of 60 degrees celsius and then let it cool before filling containers.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica creams helps with fluid retention because they contain the extract of the herb, known for its ability to improve skin's microcirculation and lymphatic drainage. brahmoside: Aids in fluid retention management by enhancing the body's natural processes to excrete excess fluids and electrolytes, promoting overall balance.

triterpenoids: Contribute to reducing fluid retention by inhibiting the activity of certain enzymes that contribute to water retention and sodium retention, thus promoting the elimination of excess fluids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Centella asiatica are commonly used in creams for fluid retention due to their high triterpenoid content, particularly asiatic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties.

remedy_recipe: Extract the centella asiatica leaves and stems using a solvent like glycerin or ethanol in a 1:10 ratio for 2 weeks.
Combine the extracted centella asiatica with a carrier oil like coconut oil in a 1:5 ratio for 24 hours.
Blend the centella asiatica mixture with a humectant like glycerin in a 1:1 ratio for 30 minutes.
Whip the mixture with a natural emulsifier like lecithin in a 1:2 ratio for 15 minutes.
Fill the centella asiatica cream into small containers and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus creams helps with fluid retention because they have diuretic properties, which help to increase urine production and reduce the amount of fluid in the body. The active compounds in Astragalus, such as saponins and polysaccharides, are believed to enhance the body's natural drainage and promote the removal of excess water and toxins. Regular use of Astragalus creams may also help to improve lymphatic circulation and reduce swelling in the body, making it an effective natural remedy for fluid retention.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of dried astragalus membranaceus root powder and 1/2 cup of beeswax for the cream base.
Combine the powder and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes to melt.
Remove the double boiler from heat and slowly add in 1/2 cup of coconut oil to emulsify the mixture.
Stir in 2 tablespoons of shea butter and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to enhance moisturizing properties.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and store in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale creams helps with fluid retention because they contain dandelion root, a natural diuretic that stimulates the kidneys to remove excess water and electrolytes from the body. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers and clean them thoroughly under cold running water for 5 minutes.
Combine 50g of the cleaned taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers with 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at a temperature of 60-70°c for 2 hours to facilitate the infusion process.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 100g of the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter in a heat-resistant mixing bowl over low heat.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ruscus aculeatus creams helps with fluid retention because they contain extracts from the Ruscus aculeatus plant, which is known for its diuretic properties. remedy_constituents: Ruscogenin: Helps with fluid retention by inhibiting the reabsorption of sodium and water in the kidneys, thereby reducing fluid accumulation in the body.

Steroidal saponins: Contribute to the reduction of fluid retention by improving lymphatic drainage and reducing the permeability of blood vessels, which helps to decrease fluid accumulation in the tissues.
Coumarins: Aid in reducing fluid retention by inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators, which can contribute to fluid accumulation and swelling in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ruscus aculeatus are commonly used due to their diuretic properties, which help to increase urine production and reduce fluid retention.

Stems: The stems of Ruscus aculeatus are used due to their ability to stimulate diuresis, helping to eliminate excess fluids from the body and alleviate fluid retention symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ruscus aculeatus leaves in the early morning when the dew is still present on the leaves for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm room with a temperature of 35-40 degrees celsius for 7-10 days.
Grind 30 grams of dried ruscus aculeatus leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler at a temperature of 60-70 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and transfer the resulting herbal cream to a glass jar for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus creams helps with fluid retention because of its diuretic properties, which help to increase urine production and reduce water retention in the body. The cream contains mucilage, a soothing and protective agent that helps to calm the skin and improve its ability to eliminate fluids. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties help to reduce swelling and promote the drainage of excess fluids, making it an effective remedy for alleviating fluid retention symptoms. remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help reduce fluid retention by exerting a mild diuretic effect, promoting the removal of excess water and electrolytes from the body.
Triterpenoids (saponins): Saponins in Verbascum thapsus help minimize fluid retention by reducing the absorption of sodium ions, which in turn decreases water retention in the body.

Phenolic acids (flavonoids): Flavonoids in the plant contribute to the reduction of fluid retention by exerting a diuretic effect and improving kidney function, thereby enhancing the removal of excess fluids from the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their diuretic properties, which help reduce fluid retention.
Flowers: Used for their anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, which also aid in reducing fluid retention.
Roots: Used for their diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate symptoms of fluid retention.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with fluid retention because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe barbadensis plant contains glycoproteins and polysaccharides, which help to reduce inflammation and improve lymphatic circulation. This improved circulation enables the body to remove excess fluids and toxins more efficiently, reducing the appearance of swelling and puffiness. Regular use of Aloe barbadensis creams can help to maintain a healthy fluid balance, promoting a more toned and refreshed appearance.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds have a diuretic effect, increasing urine production and helping the body eliminate excess fluids and electrolytes.
Aloin: As a laxative and diuretic, aloin helps to increase urine production and reduce fluid retention by stimulating the kidneys to work more efficiently.
Saponins: The saponins in Aloe barbadensis help to reduce fluid retention by increasing the excretion of sodium and water in the urine, which in turn reduces swelling and edema.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of aloe vera gel, which has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that help reduce fluid retention.

Gel extracted from Leaves: The gel extracted from the leaves is particularly effective in reducing fluid retention due to its high content of glycoproteins, which help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation.

Aloe Vera Extract from Leaves: Aloe vera extract from the leaves is also widely used due to its ability to improve skin elasticity and reduce water retention, making it an effective ingredient in creams for fluid retention.
remedy_recipe: Melt 1 ounce of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for about 10 minutes.
Add 2 ounces of coconut oil and 2 ounces of shea butter to the melted beeswax.
Remove the mixture from heat and stir in 2 tablespoons of aloe barbadensis gel.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for about 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it becomes light and fluffy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago lanceolata creams helps with fluid retention because they are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds and flavonoids. The extract of Plantago lanceolata has diuretic properties, which help in increasing the production of urine and promoting the removal of excess fluids from the body. The cream also contains soothing and calming agents that help to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms associated with fluid retention, such as puffiness and discomfort. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to manage fluid retention and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Apolactone: Helps with fluid retention by reducing water absorption in the body and improving the function of the kidneys.
Phenolic acids (such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid): Assist in reducing fluid retention by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation in the body.
Flavonoids (such as luteolin): Help with fluid retention by reducing the permeability of blood vessels and improving the removal of excess fluids from the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their astringent and diuretic properties, which help to reduce water retention in the body.
Stems: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, which aid in relieving fluid retention and reducing swelling.
Roots: They are used due to their diuretic and expectorant properties, which help to increase urine production and alleviate fluid retention in the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh plantago lanceolata leaves and flowers when they are in full bloom.
Steep the plantago lanceolata leaves and flowers in 500 milliliters of boiling distilled water for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat until melted.
Combine the strained plantago lanceolata infusion with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture and pour into jars.
